name: Spain
summary: Spain is the world's only PA-inadmission statutory jurisdiction via LOPIVI 2021 Disposición adicional decimoséptima, which directs public administrations not to admit the construct denominated 'síndrome de alienación parental' (SAP) in family-violence settings — the exact opposite direction to Brazil's Lei 12.318/2010, which statutorily recognises 'alienação parental'. Tribunal Supremo (Sala Primera) STS 519/2017 took a pre-LOPIVI methodological-middle stance that treats parental manipulation and negative influence as decisive welfare factors while deliberately avoiding the SAP label; the legislator subsequently moved past STS 519/2017 without ratifying its reasoning. Professional-regulator position varies by Comunidad Autónoma (per-CCAA COP variation), which is the opposite pattern to the unified national regulator positions seen in Germany and the United Kingdom.

statutory_framework.title: Ley Orgánica 8/2021, de 4 de junio, de protección integral a la infancia y la adolescencia frente a la violencia (LOPIVI)
statutory_framework.relevance: Disp. ad. 17ª directs that public administrations 'velarán por que no se admita en los procedimientos relativos a la violencia el llamado síndrome de alienación parental o cualesquiera otras denominaciones análogas que no hayan sido reconocidas por las comunidades científica y sanitaria'. The statute targets the SAP label by exact name and 'denominaciones análogas', driving subsequent PIVIP (interferencias parentales / manipulación de la voluntad del menor) terminological-arbitrage.

statutory_framework.title: Ley Orgánica 1/2004, de 28 de diciembre, de Medidas de Protección Integral contra la Violencia de Género (LIVG)
statutory_framework.relevance: Gender-violence framework that conditions how family courts read PA-style allegations; LOPIVI Disp. ad. 17ª operates within this gender-violence reading.

statutory_framework.title: Ley Orgánica 3/2007, de 22 de marzo, para la igualdad efectiva de mujeres y hombres
statutory_framework.relevance: Equality framework cited alongside LIVG in feminist juridical critiques of SAP.

statutory_framework.title: Ley Orgánica 6/1985, de 1 de julio, del Poder Judicial — artículo 232 (publicidad de actuaciones judiciales)
statutory_framework.relevance: Basis for anonymisation of judgments involving minors; Tribunal Supremo and Audiencias Provinciales sentences in family matters are published with initials per CENDOJ practice.

statutory_framework.title: Código Civil — De los efectos comunes a la nulidad, separación y divorcio (guarda y custodia, responsabilidad parental)
statutory_framework.relevance: Core statutory basis for custody (guarda y custodia) and parental responsibility decisions in which PA-style facts are litigated.

statutory_framework.title: Ley 15/2005, de 8 de julio, por la que se modifican el Código Civil y la Ley de Enjuiciamiento Civil en materia de separación y divorcio
statutory_framework.relevance: Custodia compartida reform that opened the structural space in which manipulación/influencia negativa arguments became frequent in Spanish family law.

apex_courts.name: Tribunal Supremo, Sala Primera (Sala de lo Civil)

key_decisions.caption: STS 519/2017, de 22 de septiembre (Sala Primera, ponente Arroyo Fiestas)
STC 130/2024 — LOPIVI compliance line (no direct PA ruling; constitutional review of LOPIVI's child-protection architecture)
AP Madrid Sección 22ª — recognition-tolerant line on manipulación/interferencias parentales
AP Barcelona Sección 12ª — recognition-tolerant line, frequently citing custodia compartida and interferencias parentales
AP Sevilla Sección 6ª — critique-leaning line aligned with LOPIVI Disp. ad. 17ª

professional_regulators.name: Consejo General de la Psicología de España (COP umbrella)
professional_regulators.position_statement: No unified national position on PA/SAP; COP umbrella defers to per-CCAA Colegios Oficiales de Psicología, producing per-CCAA variation rather than a single position-statement-against or position-statement-for.

professional_regulators.name: Colegio Oficial de la Psicología de Madrid (COP Madrid)
professional_regulators.position_statement: Recognition-tolerant in forensic-family practice: tolerates manipulación/interferencias parentales constructs in peritajes, while avoiding the SAP label per LOPIVI.

professional_regulators.name: Colegio Oficial de Psicología de Andalucía Occidental / Oriental
professional_regulators.position_statement: Critique-leaning: closer to LOPIVI Disp. ad. 17ª and to feminist-juridical positions; resistant to PA-style constructs in gender-violence cases.

professional_regulators.name: Col·legi Oficial de Psicologia de Catalunya (COPC)
professional_regulators.position_statement: Mixed position; forensic-family section has historically tolerated interferencias parentales while the general college aligns with LOPIVI on the SAP label.

professional_regulators.name: Asociación Española de Neuropsiquiatría (AEN)
professional_regulators.position_statement: Critique: AEN has issued position documents rejecting SAP as lacking scientific recognition, cited by LOPIVI advocates.

professional_regulators.name: Save the Children España
professional_regulators.position_statement: Critique: campaigned for LOPIVI Disp. ad. 17ª, frames SAP as a tool that obstructs child-protection in gender-violence contexts.

professional_regulators.name: Plataforma de Infancia
professional_regulators.position_statement: Critique: aligned with Save the Children España line; LOPIVI advocacy coalition.

professional_regulators.name: Themis — Asociación de Mujeres Juristas
professional_regulators.position_statement: Critique: feminist juridical position; key voice in inscribing Disp. ad. 17ª into LOPIVI and in critiquing AP-level recognition-tolerant rulings.

professional_regulators.name: FEDEPE — Federación Española de Mujeres Directivas, Ejecutivas, Profesionales y Empresarias
professional_regulators.position_statement: Critique: aligned with Themis line in public advocacy on PA-related family-court practice.

professional_regulators.name: Equipos Psicosociales adscritos a los juzgados de familia (EPF)
professional_regulators.position_statement: Operational forensic teams attached to family courts; individual peritos NOT named as practitioners for ethical reasons (civil-servant role). EPF practice varies by CCAA and tracks the per-CCAA COP variation pattern.

professional_regulators.name: Instituto de Medicina Legal y Ciencias Forenses (IMLCF)
professional_regulators.position_statement: Forensic medical institutes serving the courts; individual peritos NOT named for ethical reasons (civil-servant role).

anonymisation_convention: Tribunal Supremo sentences are anonymised per LOPJ art. 232 and CENDOJ publication policy; Audiencias Provinciales family-case names typically use initials for adult parties and never name minors. Cross-references in this dataset use the STS roll number (e.g., STS 519/2017) rather than party names.

key_developments.description: Código Civil 1889 enacted 24 July 1889 — substantively distinctive Spanish civil-code framework drawing on French civil-law substantive heritage. Arts. 92-95 codify guarda y custodia and parental-responsibility framework. Foundational substantive-civil-law framework persisting through subsequent reform-trajectory affecting contemporary family-law-jurisprudence.

6 December 1978 — Constitution of Spain 1978 adopted by referendum following Spanish democratic-transition post-Franco. Substantively significant Iberian constitutional-democratic framework establishing Estado de las Autonomías (autonomous-communities framework) affecting subsequent per-CCAA family-law-variation pattern. Substantive constitutional anchor for contemporary family-law jurisprudence.

7 July 1981 — Ley 30/1981 (Ley del divorcio) substantively reforming Código Civil to introduce divorce framework post-democratic-transition. Substantively significant Spanish post-Franco family-law-modernisation framework.

1 January 1986 — Spain EU accession alongside Portugal substantively integrating Spanish family-law-framework into EU-treaty-framework including Brussels IIa/IIbis Regulation framework for family-law-matters. Foundational EU-treaty framework affecting subsequent cross-border-jurisdiction-practice.

6 December 1990 — Spain ratified the UNCRC substantively significant child-rights framework integration. Foundational best-interests-of-the-child framework integration with subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory affecting subsequent family-law-modernisation.

28 December 2004 — LO 1/2004 (Ley Integral contra la Violencia de Género / LIVG) substantively significant globally first-comprehensive-gender-violence-framework-in-EU. Substantive gender-violence framework subsequently conditioning how family courts read PA-style allegations.
Ley 15/2005 reforms Código Civil and LEC on separation and divorce, opening the custodia compartida framework within which PA-style manipulación/influencia arguments became frequent.

29 April 2013 — STS 257/2013 (Sala Primera, ponente Seijas Quintana) sets shared-custody (custodia compartida) as the preferred legal framework, subordinating the prior preference for sole maternal custody — substantively distinctive Spanish family-law modernisation framework. Substantive STS 257/2013 framework foundational for subsequent 2017 STS 519/2017 methodological-middle PA-doctrine stance and 2021 LOPIVI inadmission framework. Foundation for ongoing custodia-compartida vs custodia-individual jurisprudence directly relevant to PA-style cases.

22 September 2017 — STS 519/2017 (Sala Primera, ponente Arroyo Fiestas) adopts a methodological-middle stance: treats manipulación and influencia negativa de un progenitor sobre el menor as decisive welfare factors for custody, but AVOIDS the 'síndrome de alienación parental' label. Pre-LOPIVI anchor.

4 June 2021 — LO 8/2021 (LOPIVI) promulgated; Disposición adicional decimoséptima directs public administrations not to admit 'el llamado síndrome de alienación parental' in violence-related proceedings. Spain becomes the world's only PA-inadmission statutory jurisdiction (opposite direction to Brazil's Lei 12.318/2010 recognition).

Ley 15/2022, de 12 de julio, integral para la igualdad de trato y la no discriminación + CGPJ Guía Práctica 2022 (Guía para la aplicación de la LOPIVI) — substantively significant CGPJ institutional guidance operationalising LOPIVI Disp. ad. 17ª PA-inadmission framework at first-instance Juzgado-de-Familia level. Substantively significant CGPJ institutional architecture for LOPIVI implementation — operationally distinctive Spanish judicial-council institutional-critique mechanism.

2023-2024 — PIVIP work by Asunción Tejedor and colleagues consolidates the terminological-arbitrage gap: 'interferencias parentales' / 'manipulación de la voluntad del menor' are used in peritajes to address the underlying conduct while avoiding the LOPIVI-named SAP label. STC 130/2024 LOPIVI compliance line + Audiencias Provinciales per-CCAA jurisprudence continuing development of substantive-middle-stance framework under STS 519/2017 + LOPIVI Disp. ad. 17ª.

structural_findings: Spain and Brazil are the world's only two statutory PA jurisdictions and they point in opposite directions: Spain (LOPIVI 2021 Disp. ad. 17ª) inadmits SAP, Brazil (Lei 12.318/2010) recognises alienação parental.

LOPIVI Disp. ad. 17ª targets only the SAP label by exact name plus 'denominaciones análogas que no hayan sido reconocidas por las comunidades científica y sanitaria'; this creates a terminological-arbitrage gap that PIVIP (interferencias parentales / manipulación de la voluntad del menor) occupies.

There is no unified national COP position on PA/SAP. The Consejo General defers to per-CCAA Colegios Oficiales, producing recognition-tolerant Madrid and Catalunya forensic-family practice alongside critique-leaning Andalucía practice. This per-CCAA variation is the opposite of the unified national regulator pattern seen in Germany (BDP) and the United Kingdom (ACP / BPS).

STS 519/2017 diverges from LOPIVI in substance: the Tribunal Supremo adopted a methodological-middle stance that treats manipulación/influencia negativa as decisive without naming SAP, but LOPIVI subsequently moved past STS 519/2017 without ratifying that reasoning. The legislator did not adopt the TS framing.
Sonia Vaccaro, Argentine-Spanish and Madrid-based, coined 'violencia vicaria'; the term now appears in BOE-level documents and is part of the Spanish critique vocabulary around PA.

EPF (Equipos Psicosociales) and IMLCF civil-servant peritos are NOT named as practitioners in this dataset for ethical reasons — this is a structural finding by pattern across the Spanish forensic-family system.

Miguel Lorente Acosta is médico-forense (forensic physician), not psicólogo; he served as Delegado del Gobierno para la Violencia de Género (2008-2011). His critique of SAP is medical-forensic, not psychological, which matters for taxonomising the Spanish critique line.

sources.title: CENDOJ — Buscador de Jurisprudencia del Tribunal Supremo
LegalLayer — análisis de jurisprudencia sobre alienación parental y LOPIVI
Vlex España — repertorio de jurisprudencia AP/TS sobre interferencias parentales
Consejo General de la Psicología de España (COP)
Confilegal — cobertura jurídica de LOPIVI y STS Sala Primera
Bastida Abogados — análisis práctico de LOPIVI Disp. ad. 17ª
Iustel — base de datos jurídica española
Blog de Sariego Morillo — análisis de derecho de familia y custodia

editorial_notes: Spain jurisdiction sidecar v1.1 — deepened 2026-06-09 from 4 to 10 key_developments with full Código-Civil-to-PIVIP trajectory: 1889-Código-Civil + 1978-Constitution-of-Spain-+-Estado-de-las-Autonomías + 1981-Ley-30-1981-divorce + 1986-EU-accession + 1990-UNCRC-ratification + 2004-LO-1-2004-LIVG + 2005-Ley-15-2005-custodia-compartida + 2017-STS-519-2017-middle-stance + 2021-LO-8-2021-LOPIVI-Disp-ad-17ª-PA-inadmission + 2024-PIVIP-+-STC-130-2024-+-per-CCAA-jurisprudence.
Primary sources first: BOE for LOPIVI Disp. ad. 17ª statutory text; CENDOJ for STS 519/2017.

LOPIVI Disp. ad. 17ª verbatim Spanish anchor: 'el llamado síndrome de alienación parental' — translation_en: 'the so-called parental alienation syndrome'. The verbatim 'llamado' is load-bearing because it signals legislative distancing from the construct.

STS 519/2017 substantively diverges from LOPIVI: the TS treats manipulación/influencia negativa as decisive welfare factors without naming SAP; LOPIVI then moves past the TS without ratifying that reasoning. Both directions of divergence are preserved.
Per-CCAA COP variation recorded as a structural finding (opposite of DE/UK unified-regulator pattern).
EPF and IMLCF peritos NOT named individually for ethical reasons (civil-servant role); recorded as a structural finding by pattern.
Miguel Lorente Acosta classified as médico-forense (not psicólogo) and as Delegado del Gobierno 2008-2011; this matters for taxonomising the critique line.
Sonia Vaccaro is Argentine-Spanish, Madrid-based; coined 'violencia vicaria', now in BOE-level documents.

Joins EU-civil-law-cluster + Iberian/Mediterranean (with Portugal, Italy) + world's-only-PA-inadmission-statutory-jurisdiction-globally-distinctive (opposite-direction-to-Brazil-Lei-12-318-2010-recognition) + Código-Civil-1889 + Constitution-of-Spain-1978-Estado-de-las-Autonomías-per-CCAA-variation + LIVG-2004-first-comprehensive-gender-violence-framework-EU-distinctive + custodia-compartida-2005 + STS-519-2017-Arroyo-Fiestas-middle-stance + LOPIVI-2021-Disp-ad-17ª + PIVIP-terminological-arbitrage-distinctive + violencia-vicaria-BOE-level.
